will he dispelled by the rays of gems, when the Sacred Fire is of the
hue of gold, A^oka flower, Kuranta flower, lotus, beryl or blue lily.
The idea is that if the Fire shows the above characteristics, the
king will soon conquer the entire earth and become very wealthy
and happy.
[These verses give an opportunity to the author to show off
his poetic powers. The metre is the usual There is also
the figure of speech named ^TSxrftr^.]

Those kings whose sacrificial fire emits sound similar to that
of a group of chariots, oceans, clouds, elephants or drums, will in
their march darken the quarters, being crowded with hosts of intoxi-
cated elephants.
[In the previous verse the author refers to the auspicious
colours of the Fire. Here he refers to its auspicious sounds. The
figures are quite patent.
The metre is an of the ^sT^STr and mixed
together.]
